refs.title: claude-hud/CLAUDE.README.md at main · jarrodwatts ... - GitHub
refs.snippet: Claude HUD Real-time statusline showing context usage, active tools, running agents, and todo progress. Claude Code invokes the plugin → Plugin reads JSON from stdin (model, context, tokens) → Plugin parses transcript JSONL file (tools, agents, todos) → Plugin reads config files (MCPs, hooks, rules) → Plugin renders lines to stdout → Claude Code displays the statusline. Plugin not installed or statusLine not configured Run: /plugin marketplace add jarrodwatts/claude-hud Run: /plugin install claude-hud Run: /claude-hud:setup Ensure Claude Code is v1.0.80 or later No stdin data received (normal on first invocation) This is expected briefly on startup, should resolve automatically Data comes directly from Claude Code - it's accurate The percentage is (input\_tokens / context\_window\_size) \* 100 No tools used yet or transcript not being parsed Lines only appear when there's data to show Ensure Node.js 18+ installed Run npm ci to reinstall dependencies Delete dist/ and node\_modules/, then npm ci && npm run build.

# Claude HUD A Claude Code plugin that shows what's happening — context usage, active tools, running agents, and todo progress. Always visible below your input.  ...    Inside a Claude Code instance, run the following commands:

**Step 1: Add the marketplace** ```
/plugin marketplace add jarrodwatts/claude-hud
```

**Step 2: Install the plugin**  ...    Claude HUD gives you better insights into what's happening in your Claude Code session.  ...    | **Project path** | Know which project you're in (configurable 1-3 directory levels) |  ...    ```json
{
  "lineLayout": "expanded",
  "pathLevels": 2,  ...    ---

## Requirements

- Claude Code v1.0.80+
- Node.js 18+ or Bun

---

## Development ```bash
git clone https://github.com/jarrodwatts/claude-hud
cd claude-hud
npm ci && npm run build
npm test
``` See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

---

refs.title: Releases · gsd-build/get-shit-done - GitHub
refs.snippet: *   **`/gsd:ship` command** — PR creation from verified phase work with auto-generated PR body ([#829](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/829)). *   **`/gsd:next` command** — Automatic workflow advancement to the next logical step ([#927](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/927)). *   **Cross-phase regression gate** — Execute-phase runs prior phases' test suites after execution ([#945](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/945)). *   **Requirements coverage gate** — Plan-phase verifies all phase requirements are covered ([#984](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/984)). *   **Codex hooks support** — SessionStart hook support for Codex runtime ([#1020](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/1020)). *   PROJECT.md drift and phase completion counter accuracy ([#956](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/956)). *   Copilot executor stuck issue — runtime compatibility fallback ([#1128](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/1128)). *   CRLF frontmatter parsing, duplicate cwd crash, STATE.md phase transitions ([#1105](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/pull/1105)). *   Phase-complete properly updates REQUIREMENTS.md traceability (closes [#848](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/848)). *   Empty-answer validation guards added to discuss-phase (closes [#912](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/912)). *   Windows: `@file:` protocol resolution for large init payloads (>50KB) — all 32 workflow/agent files now resolve temp file paths instead of letting agents hallucinate `/tmp` paths ([#841](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/841)). *   Installer now replaces `$HOME/.claude/` paths (not just `~/.claude/`) for non-Claude runtimes — fixes broken commands on local installs and Gemini/OpenCode/Codex installs ([#905](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/905), [#909](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done/issues/909)).

refs.title: Plan Mode Isn't Enough. GSD is the Framework I Actually Use
refs.snippet:
Plan Mode Isn't Enough. GSD is the Framework I Actually Use
JeredBlu
11800 subscribers
47 likes
1186 views
14 Feb 2026
GSD (Get Shit Done) is the spec-driven development framework I've been using for over a month — and it's my favorite one yet. It combines SDD with context engineering so you never hit context rot.

In this video, I'll walk you through what makes GSD different from other frameworks like Superpowers, BMAD, and SpecKit. GSD breaks your project into phases, each in a fresh context window, finishing at ~50% context so you never get degraded outputs.

I'll cover the full workflow: PRD → roadmap 
→ discussion → research → plan → execute → verify, and show you how GSD's persistent state lets you clear context and pick up exactly where you left off.

⌚ TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 - What is GSD?
00:28 - Why planning matters before building
01:10 - What spec-driven development does
01:45 - What sets GSD apart (context engineering)
02:15 - Starting a new project
02:45 - Discussion and research phases
03:20 - Plan and execute phases
03:50 - Verify and approve workflow
04:15 - GSD file structure
04:35 - Agent compatibility
05:00 - Pause and resume workflow
05:30 - Testing recommendations
06:00 - Final thoughts

🔗 RELEVANT LINKS:
GSD Framework → https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done
My PRD Creator Skill (Free) → https://github.com/JeredBlu/jeredblu-marketplace
Book a call with me → https://yedatechs.com/#discovery-call
Sponsorship inquiries → hi@yedatechs.com

#GSD #SpecDrivenDevelopment #SDD #ClaudeCode #AICoding #ContextEngineering #AIFramework #DevWorkflow #OpenCode #GeminiCLI

refs.title: GET SH*T DONE: Meta-prompting and Spec-driven Development for Claude Code and Codex
refs.snippet: In-depth explanation of GSD framework: forces work into small checkable plans in fresh context windows with atomic git commits, discuss phase, and Nyquist validation to prevent agent chaos.

refs.title: Get Shit Done - Get Shit Done
refs.snippet:
A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven development framework for AI code assistants Get Shit Done A light-weight and powerful meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven development framework for Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and Codex. 
Get Started



View on GitHub


## What is GSD? Get Shit Done (GSD) is a context engineering layer that makes Claude Code reliable. It solves context rot — the quality  ...    Parallel specialized agents handle research, planning, execution, and verification

## Spec-Driven Development  ...    GSD was built by a solo developer who doesn't write code — Claude Code does. Other spec-driven tools add complexity  ...    Trusted by engineers at Amazon, Google, Shopify, and Webflow.

## Quick Start

```bash
npx get-shit-done-cc@latest ```

```bash
npx get-shit-done-cc@latest

```

The installer prompts you to choose:  ...    - Claude Code / Gemini: `/gsd:help`
- OpenCode: `/gsd-help`
- Codex: `$gsd-help`

## Initialize Your Project Run `/gsd:new-project`

refs.title: ClawHub - Skill Directory for OpenClaw - GitHub
refs.snippet: ClawHub is the **public skill registry for Clawdbot**: publish, version, and search text-based agent skills (a `SKILL.md` plus supporting files). onlycrabs.ai is the **SOUL.md registry**: publish and share system lore the same way you publish skills. * Canonicalize owned skills: `clawhub skill rename` , `clawhub skill merge`. * Uploaded registry skills use soft-delete/restore (`clawhub delete`  / `clawhub undelete`  or API equivalents). # edit .env.local — see CONTRIBUTING.md for local Convex values ## terminal A: local Convex backend ## terminal B: web app (port 3000) # # seed sample data #. ClawHub can store a nix-clawdbot plugin pointer in SKILL frontmatter so the registry knows which Nix package bundle to install. A nix plugin is different from a regular skill pack: it bundles the skill pack, the CLI binary, and its config flags/requirements together. Add this to `SKILL.md`:. Skills declare their runtime requirements (env vars, binaries, install specs) in the `SKILL.md` frontmatter. ClawHub's security analysis checks these declarations against actual skill behavior.

refs.title: Gog - ClawHub
refs.snippet: # Gog. Google Workspace CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Contacts, Sheets, and Docs. Like a lobster shell, security has layers — review code before you run it. ### License. ### Runtime requirements. ### Install. `brew install steipete/tap/gogcli`. ## SKILL.md. # gog. Use `gog` for Gmail/Calendar/Drive/Contacts/Sheets/Docs. `gog auth credentials /path/to/client_secret.json`. `gog auth add you@gmail.com --services gmail,calendar,drive,contacts,sheets,docs`. `gog gmail search 'newer_than:7d' --max 10`. `gog gmail send --to a@b.com --subject "Hi" --body "Hello"`. `gog calendar events <calendarId> --from <iso> --to <iso>`. `gog drive search "query" --max 10`. `gog contacts list --max 20`. `gog sheets get <sheetId> "Tab! `gog sheets update <sheetId> "Tab! A1:B2" --values-json '[["A","B"],["1","2"]]' --input USER_ENTERED`. `gog sheets append <sheetId> "Tab! A:C" --values-json '' --insert INSERT_ROWS`. `gog sheets clear <sheetId> "Tab! `gog sheets metadata <sheetId> --json`. `gog docs export <docId> --format txt --out /tmp/doc.txt`. ### Files. ## Comments.

refs.title: skills/skills/halthelobster/proactive-agent/SKILL.md at main - GitHub
refs.snippet: * **Working Buffer** — Survive the danger zone between memory flush and compaction. ✅ **Working Buffer** — Captures every exchange in the danger zone. workspace/ ├── ONBOARDING.md # First-run setup (tracks progress) ├── AGENTS.md # Operating rules, learned lessons, workflows ├── SOUL.md # Identity, principles, boundaries ├── USER.md # Human's context, goals, preferences ├── MEMORY.md # Curated long-term memory ├── SESSION-STATE.md # ⭐ Active working memory (WAL target) ├── HEARTBEAT.md # Periodic self-improvement checklist ├── TOOLS.md # Tool configurations, gotchas, credentials └── memory/ ├── YYYY-MM-DD.md # Daily raw capture └── working-buffer.md # ⭐ Danger zone log. 2. **WRITE** — Update SESSION-STATE.md with the detail. 1. **FIRST:** Read `memory/working-buffer.md` — raw danger-zone exchanges. 2. **SECOND:** Read `SESSION-STATE.md` — active task state. **Do NOT ask "what were we discussing?"** — the working buffer literally has the conversation. * **Docs** — TOOLS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, AGENTS.md. 3. Check automated jobs — next cron run should use new tool.

refs.title: ClawHub - OpenClaw
refs.snippet:
ClawHub ClawHub is the public skill registry for OpenClaw. It is a free service: all skills are public, open, and visible to everyone for sharing and reuse. A skill is just a folder with a `SKILL.md` file (plus supporting text files). You can browse skills in the web app or use the CLI to search, install, update, and publish skills. Site: clawhub.ai

## What ClawHub is - A public registry for OpenClaw skills.
- A versioned store of skill bundles and metadata.  ...    1. A user publishes a skill bundle (files + metadata).  ...    If you want to add new capabilities to your OpenClaw agent, ClawHub is the easiest way to find and install skills. You  ...    configured, `clawhub` falls back to that workspace unless you override `--workdir` (or `CLAWHUB_WORKDIR`). OpenClaw  ...    ClawHub uses metadata to power discovery and safely expose skill capabilities.  ...    ClawHub is open by default. Anyone can upload skills, but a GitHub account must  ...    ## Common workflows for agents
